name: Home Assistant Vibecode Agent
what_it_does: Home Assistant add-on and MCP server that lets AI IDEs manage smart home configurations through natural language. Provides real-time access to entities, automations, dashboards, and HACS integrations with Git-based version control and rollback.
use_cases: Creating complex Home Assistant automations via natural language
Building Lovelace dashboards programmatically through AI
Managing HACS integrations and add-ons via MCP
Debugging and validating Home Assistant configurations
Version-controlled smart home config management with rollback
not_for: Users without existing Home Assistant installations
Beginners unfamiliar with Home Assistant concepts
Production-critical home security systems without manual review
best_when: You have a mature Home Assistant setup and want AI-assisted configuration management with safety nets like Git versioning and config validation.
avoid_when: You are new to Home Assistant, need guaranteed reliability for safety-critical automations, or want a standalone smart home controller.

auth.notes: Bearer token authentication required for all API endpoints except /api/health. Token generated during add-on configuration.

pricing.notes: MIT license. Free and open source. Requires existing Home Assistant installation.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Config validation before apply is a strong safety feature. Swagger/ReDoc API docs available. Error handling for HA API failures unclear.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Entity creation may not be idempotent, but Git versioning provides rollback safety net.
known_agent_gotchas: Two-component architecture: add-on in HA + MCP server on local machine
AI can accidentally break Home Assistant configurations
File access restricted to /config directory only
NPM package @coolver/home-assistant-mcp needed for IDE integration
